plan_metadata.name: group  validation Sample Npm Search Connector

steps.description: Click the "Search (Ctrl+Alt+E)" input box at the top of the Microsoft Teams web interface to activate search functionality.
Type the text 'haolong' into the search bar at the top of the Microsoft Teams web interface to initiate a search within chats, messages, files, images, group chats, or meetings.
Click on the "Long Hao" search result in the Microsoft Teams web interface to select the user from the search dropdown.
Click the "Actions and apps" button (red plus icon) at the bottom right of the chat input area in the Microsoft Teams web interface to open app and messaging extension options.
Click the "Search for apps" input field in the "Get more apps" popup within the Apps section of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Type text ${{var:app_name}}  into the "Get more apps" search field in the Microsoft Teams web interface, located within the Chat tab conversation view.
@assertion there's ${{var:app_name}}dev or ${{var:app_name}}local in search result list.
Click the ${{var:app_name}} search result entry in the pop-up list after searching for "npmsearch" within the Microsoft Teams desktop chat interface.
Click the "Your Search query" input field within the "npmSearchConnector" popup window in the Microsoft Teams web interface, allowing the user to enter a search term.
Type the text '@microsoft/teamsapp' into the "Your search query" input field of the npmSearchConnector popup within the Microsoft Teams chat interface (web version).

Click the first search result "@microsoft/teamsapp-cli" in the "npmSearchConnector" pop-up within the Microsoft Teams web application chat interface, selecting the highlighted package from the suggestion dropdown.
Click the "Send" button located at the bottom right of the Microsoft Teams chat interface to submit a message in a conversation.
